diagrams.title: Five State Process Model
diagrams.description: Shows the five basic process states and transitions between them
elements: Transitions: New→Ready, Ready→Running, Running→Ready, Running→Waiting, Waiting→Ready, Running→Terminated
diagrams.purpose: Understand how processes move between states during their lifetime and what causes each transition

diagrams.title: Process Control Block Structure
diagrams.description: Data structure storing complete information about a process
elements: Memory Management Info (base/limit registers, page tables)
diagrams.purpose: Visualize what information the OS maintains for each process and why context switching requires saving all this data

diagrams.title: Memory Layout of Process Image
diagrams.description: Shows how process memory is organized in RAM
elements: High Addresses: Command-line arguments and environment variables
Stack (grows downward): Function calls, local variables, return addresses
Data segment: Initialized and uninitialized global/static variables
Text segment (low addresses): Program code (machine instructions)
diagrams.purpose: Understand process memory organization and how different parts of program are stored and accessed
